title: Deferring to Trump, G.O.P. Lawmakers Resist a Public Accounting on Iran
summary: Nearly three weeks into a war that polls show is unpopular, top Republicans have yet to call administration officials to testify about it, arguing that hearings would put divisions on display.

title: Iran War Underscores the Diverging Aims of Trump and Netanyahu
summary: The United States views Iran through a prism of global responsibilities and strategic goals. Israel has a more regional approach. After nearly three weeks of war, their paths are diverging.

title: A Diary of War From an Unlikely Author: the Son of Iran’s President
summary: In an online journal, Yousef Pezeshkian offers readers a mix of personal anecdotes and glimpses behind the scenes as Iranian leaders are picked off one after another.

title: U.S. Prosecutors Are Investigating Colombia President Gustavo Petro
summary: The Justice Department is examining whether President Gustavo Petro of Colombia has ties to drug traffickers.

title: Trump Administration Sues Harvard Over Accusations of Antisemitism
summary: The administration had spent months investigating the Ivy League school. The two sides had been in talks to negotiate a settlement.

title: Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
summary: Paolo Zampolli, a former modeling agent and a longtime Trump ally, was in a custody battle over his son. An ICE official agreed to help.

title: New Data Shows Where ICE Has Been Most Active This Year
summary: The pace of ICE arrests nationwide has topped 1,100 per day on average in 2026, but the rate of arrests has varied across the country in sometimes surprising ways.

title: Chuck Norris, Action Star of ‘Walker, Texas Ranger,’ Dies at 86
summary: He channeled his martial arts skills into heroic roles in films like “The Delta Force” and “Missing in Action” and in the long-running TV series “Walker, Texas Ranger.”

title: Cesar Chavez Avenue May Soon Be Gone. Yet to Be Confronted: His Legacy.
summary: After the revelations of sex abuse, the public is left to make sense of the labor leader’s work and life.

title: How a Civil Rights Icon Used His Power to Abuse Girls and Women
summary: For five years, our reporters Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes followed allegations that the civil rights icon Cesar Chavez had sexually abused girls and women at all levels of the United Farm Workers movement. Manny Fernandez takes us inside their investigation.

title: Presidents Who Honored Cesar Chavez Have Yet to React to Abuse Allegations
summary: Bill Clinton gave Mr. Chavez the nation’s highest civilian honor. Barack Obama made Cesar Chavez Day a federal holiday. Joe Biden displayed a bronze bust of Mr. Chavez. So far, all have been quiet.

title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
summary: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.

title: He Spent Five Years in Solitary. He Came Out a Jailhouse Lawyer.
summary: Obsessed with proving his innocence, Quentin Lewis devoted years in isolation to learning the law. Now he is taking on his captors in prison tribunals.

title: BTS Is Back With ‘Arirang,’ but the K-Pop Landscape Has Changed
summary: The superstar boy band returns after a four-year hiatus on Friday. The genre it helped turn into a global juggernaut has endured some shifts, and minted new stars.

title: Lights, Drones, Purple Pizza: BTS-mania Takes Over Seoul
summary: The South Korean capital was aglow for the return of the K-pop supergroup.

title: What To Know About the BTS Comeback Concert Streaming Live on Netflix
summary: The K-pop supergroup’s first show in over three years will stream live on Netflix at 7 a.m. Eastern time on Saturday. The New York Times will cover it live from Seoul.

title: A.I. Is Writing Fiction. Publishers Are Unprepared.
summary: Book publishing has few safeguards in place to prevent the unwitting publication of a novel heavily generated by artificial intelligence.

title: More! More! More! Tech Workers Max Out Their A.I. Use.
summary: At a number of companies, employees compete on leaderboards to show how much A.I. they’re using. They’re racking up big bills along the way.

title: A Meatpacking Strike in Colorado Is Another Stress to Trump’s Economy
summary: The walkout at the JBS plant in Greeley, Colo., comes as beef prices have soared, cattle supplies have plunged and immigration sweeps have stressed the labor supply.

title: Alexander Brothers Are Said to Seek Trump Pardons for Sex Trafficking
summary: People representing the three brothers have reached out to a lobbyist and an influential Jewish group that have supported clemency for others. There are no signs the White House is considering a pardon.

title: Blood Plasma Centers Move Into More Middle-Class Neighborhoods
summary: Across the United States, plasma centers are opening in wealthier areas as more people struggle with the high cost of housing, groceries and health care.

title: Record Number of Student Loan Borrowers Are in Delinquency and Default
summary: Recently released data from the Education Department showed that by the end of last year, 7.7 million borrowers had defaulted on $181 billion in federal student loans.

title: CBS News Radio, Onetime Home of Murrow, Will End Amid Layoffs
summary: Bari Weiss, the editor in chief of CBS News, announced layoffs on Friday that affected more than 60 employees. The network is owned by the technology heir David Ellison.

title: How Muslim Businesses in New York Prepare to Celebrate Eid
summary: From Fordham to Bay Ridge to Hollis, Muslim businesses get ready for Eid al-Fitr, the exultant celebration marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

title: Supreme Court Allows Street Preacher’s Lawsuit
summary: Gabriel Olivier was arrested after violating an ordinance restricting demonstrations outside an amphitheater in Brandon, Miss.

title: What the ‘Project Hail Mary’ Directors Learned From a Firing
summary: Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, best known for animations like the “Spider-Verse” films, took lessons from “Solo: A Star Wars Story,” a project from which they were dismissed.

title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Review: Ryan Gosling Is Lost and Found in Space
summary: The actor plays a molecular biologist trying to help save the world in this upbeat science-fiction fantasy from Phil Lord and Christopher Miller.

title: Andy Weir on Writing the Hit Book Behind the Movie ‘Project Hail Mary’
summary: The author talked about adapting his best-selling novel for film, creating the beloved character Rocky and making complex science feel approachable.

title: ‘Once and for All’ Means Never
summary: Hamas has survived waves of killings of its leaders. More than force will be necessary to overturn the Islamic regime of Iran.

title: We’re All Living in the ‘Mirror World’ Now
summary: Naomi Klein and Ezra Klein discuss what the success of MAGA reveals about the American left.

title: Whose War?
summary: We look at the diverging goals of the U.S. and Israel in the war on Iran.

title: Have You Turned to A.I. For Advice on a Romantic Relationship?
summary: People are using generative A.I. chatbots for help on everything from starting to ending a relationship. Have you? How did it turn out?

title: Hochul Moves to Delay and Alter Climate Law, Citing Energy Prices
summary: The proposal, currently being considered by the Legislature during budget talks, would put off regulations for enforcing the law until 2030.

title: A Meningitis Outbreak at the University of Kent Evokes Covid Memories
summary: Residents and students in Canterbury, where the outbreak was centered, said measures to contain the disease were both familiar and frightening.

title: Lukashenko Jailed Her in Belarus, but She Wants the World to Talk to Him
summary: Maria Kalesnikava is campaigning for the West to engage with the regime in Belarus that imprisoned her for more than five years.

title: Calvin Tomkins, Who Profiled Giants of Modern Art, Dies at 100
summary: On the staff of The New Yorker for more than 60 years, he wrote about Duchamp, Rauschenberg and many others. His books include “Living Well Is the Best Revenge.”
